headline: Musk's OpenAI case runs out of time
subtitle: Musk loses OpenAI lawsuit · Cursor's Composer 2.5 nears frontier · Odyssey's multiplayer world models
intro: After three weeks of high-profile testimonies and a $100B lawsuit, Elon Musk's case against OpenAI was dismissed on technical grounds when jurors ruled it was filed years too late. The blockbuster trial ended with a legal dismissal rather than a decision on the merits, leaving the fundamental questions about AI governance unsettled.

bullets.text: Musk loses OpenAI lawsuit on statute of limitations grounds
bullets.summary: A jury unanimously dismissed Musk's $100B+ lawsuit against OpenAI, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and Microsoft, finding the case was filed years too late. The lawsuit alleged the executives converted a nonprofit into a for-profit entity, but the jury determined Musk knew about the transition long before suing. Musk called it a calendar technicality and vowed to appeal.

bullets.text: Cursor releases Composer 2.5 with frontier-class coding performance
bullets.summary: Cursor's upgraded in-house coding model achieves near-frontier benchmark performance at significantly lower costs. Composer 2.5 reaches Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 level scores while costing under $1 per task, compared to $11 for competing models. The model was partially trained on xAI's Colossus 2 compute infrastructure.

bullets.text: Odyssey launches first real-time multimodal and multiplayer world models
bullets.summary: Odyssey released Starchild-1, a real-time multimodal world model generating synchronized audio and video that adapts to user inputs without fixed length constraints. The company also unveiled Agora-1, enabling up to four players to interact simultaneously in an AI-generated world while maintaining shared game state.

bullets.text: Anthropic acquires Stainless, maker of Claude SDKs and tools
bullets.summary: Anthropic acquired Stainless, the startup responsible for building and maintaining Claude's official software development kits and MCP server tooling. The acquisition brings the developer-focused team in-house to strengthen infrastructure around Claude's ecosystem.

bullets.text: Amazon launches Alexa Podcasts, AI-generated custom audio shows
bullets.summary: Amazon rolled out Alexa Podcasts for Alexa+ subscribers, a NotebookLM-style tool that generates conversations between two AI hosts on any topic. The feature allows users to create custom podcast content without manual production work.

bullets.text: OpenAI partners with Malta for free ChatGPT Plus nationwide
bullets.summary: OpenAI announced a country-wide partnership with Malta offering free ChatGPT Plus access to every citizen who completes a national AI literacy course. This marks the first country-level deal of its kind combining AI education with platform access.

bullets.text: Alibaba's Qwen 3.7 model preview now available on Arena
bullets.summary: Alibaba released a preview version of its Qwen 3.7 model on Arena, allowing developers to test the latest iteration of their language model capabilities and compare performance across benchmarks.

bullets.text: Cursor releases Composer 2.5 with enhanced code generation
bullets.summary: Cursor launched Composer 2.5, an updated version of its AI coding assistant that improves code generation and editor integration, making it easier for developers to leverage AI in their workflows.

bullets.text: Anthropic acquires Stainless to strengthen SDK development
bullets.summary: Anthropic acquired Stainless, an SDK development startup, to enhance its API and developer tooling capabilities. This strategic move aims to improve how developers integrate Claude into their applications.

bullets.text: Jury dismisses all claims in Musk's lawsuit against Altman
bullets.summary: A jury completely dismissed all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, concluding a legal dispute that had raised questions about governance and business dealings at the AI company.

bullets.text: NVIDIA's robot video generation model gets fine-tuning guide
bullets.summary: NVIDIA published documentation on fine-tuning Cosmos Predict 2.5 using LoRA and DoRA techniques for generating robot training videos, enabling researchers to customize video generation for robotics applications.

bullets.text: NVIDIA Vera CPU for agents arrives at AI research labs
bullets.summary: NVIDIA's Vera CPU, specifically designed to power AI agents, has been delivered to leading AI research institutions. The hardware aims to optimize performance for agent-based AI workloads.

bullets.text: Codex now lets you control Mac tasks from your phone
bullets.summary: Codex updated to enable phone-based control of Mac, devbox, or remote machine work while keeping files and credentials secure. You can approve commands, answer questions, and review diffs from your phone while execution happens elsewhere. The update also introduces Hooks to Codex.

bullets.text: Cloudflare tests Mythos, finds smart models need good harnesses
bullets.summary: Cloudflare tested Anthropic's Mythos against 50 repos and found it excels at spotting real attacks involving chained vulnerabilities. However, a smart model alone without proper orchestration misses significant issues. Teams should focus on making bugs harder to chain rather than just finding them faster.

bullets.text: Cursor Composer 2.5 reaches Opus/GPT-5.5 performance cheaper
bullets.summary: Cursor released Composer 2.5, partly trained on SpaceX GPUs, achieving similar benchmarks to Opus 4.7-xhigh and GPT-5.5-high at significantly lower cost. The update aims to bring cutting-edge coding capability to developers at scale.

bullets.text: Linear Agent reads codebases to investigate bugs and features
bullets.summary: Linear Agent now directly analyzes repository code to build hypotheses, investigate support issues, find original contributors, and provide deeper context. This tighter integration between issue tracking and code understanding improves debugging workflow.

bullets.text: Browse.sh offers open-source skills for agent web automation
bullets.summary: Browserbase released Browse.sh, an open-source catalogue of reusable skills and playbooks enabling agents to perform complex tasks on the internet. This modular approach lets developers compose behaviors without rebuilding from scratch.

bullets.text: Musk's OpenAI lawsuit dismissed over missed deadline
bullets.summary: After weeks of courtroom drama, a federal jury ruled Musk's claims were filed three years too late under statute of limitations law. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers accepted the verdict, and Musk plans to appeal, calling the ruling a calendar technicality rather than a substantive legal loss.

bullets.text: Microsoft opens ECHO for agents to learn from failures
bullets.summary: Microsoft open-sourced ECHO, a system teaching terminal agents to predict environmental outcomes from their own failed commands. This focus on reliability moves beyond simply iterating until tests pass toward genuine cause-and-effect understanding.

bullets.text: Odyssey launches live-world video generation tools
bullets.summary: Odyssey introduced Starchild-1 for real-time audio and video world simulation, plus Agora-1 for shared multiplayer environments. The shift moves generated media from passive viewing toward interactive steering.

bullets.text: OpenAI connects ChatGPT to bank accounts for US Pro users
bullets.summary: OpenAI launched ChatGPT personal finance using Plaid for bank connections, enabling more contextual advice but raising intimacy and security concerns around storing sensitive financial habits and obligations.

bullets.text: Public trust in AI eroding over jobs and power impacts
bullets.summary: Axios polling reveals AI backlash extending beyond abstract concerns into concrete worries about employment, electricity costs, surveillance, and local political power. Communities are becoming harder to convince on data center approvals.

main_story.title: Musk's OpenAI lawsuit dismissed on technical grounds
main_story.body: Elon Musk's massive $100B+ lawsuit against OpenAI, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and Microsoft ended after a three-week trial when a jury unanimously ruled the case was filed years too late. The lawsuit alleged that Altman and Brockman improperly converted a nonprofit organization into a for-profit entity, but the jury determined Musk had known about this transition long before filing suit. OpenAI's attorneys successfully argued that Musk himself had backed the for-profit structure, pushed for control, and only sued after starting his own AI rival xAI in 2023. Musk responded on X by characterizing the verdict as a mere calendar technicality unrelated to the merits of his claims and announced plans to appeal the decision.
main_story.why: This outcome leaves fundamental questions about nonprofit-to-for-profit conversion, governance of AI organizations, and stakeholder rights unresolved despite the high-profile trial. The case failed to set legal precedent or clarify the rules around how AI companies can transition structures when billions of dollars are involved.
main_story.take: While OpenAI secured a decisive victory, the dismissal on technical grounds rather than substance means the broader debate about AI governance and founder accountability remains unfinished business. Musk's promised appeal could eventually bring these issues back to court on the merits.

around_the_horn.text: Meta begins layoffs affecting up to 8,000 employees and cancels 6,000 open roles
OpenAI partners with Dell to deploy Codex inside enterprise data centers
Krea releases Krea 2, its first in-house image generation model

previous.headline: OpenAI takes Codex mobile with ChatGPT app
previous.subtitle: Codex now on iOS · Apple partnership deteriorates · Anthropic angers devs
previous.intro: OpenAI rolled out Codex in preview on iOS, letting developers manage long-running AI tasks from their phones while agents work remotely. Meanwhile, tensions with Apple over ChatGPT-Siri integration are escalating, and Anthropic's new agent credit system is sparking subscription cancellations.

bullets.text: OpenAI launches Codex on iOS for managing AI agents remotely
bullets.summary: Codex now runs in preview on ChatGPT's iOS app across all plans, allowing developers to track and manage long-running AI tasks, approve decisions, and start new tasks from their phones while agents continue working on desktops or remote hosts. The system uses a secure relay layer that doesn't expose computers to the internet and syncs across ChatGPT instances.

bullets.text: OpenAI weighs legal action against Apple over failing ChatGPT deal
bullets.summary: OpenAI is considering legal action, including potential breach-of-contract claims, over Apple's 2024 ChatGPT-Siri integration. OpenAI expected billions in paid signups but found users preferred the standalone app over Apple's limited integration. Apple plans to open Siri to rival providers like Claude and Gemini in iOS 27 and has expressed frustration over OpenAI recruiting from its hardware teams.

bullets.text: Anthropic splits agent usage into monthly credit pools, sparking backlash
bullets.summary: Starting June 15, Anthropic is moving Agent SDK and Claude code generation into separate monthly credit pools, no longer drawing from subscription limits. Pro users get $20/month, Max 5x gets $100, and Max 20x gets $200, with credits not rolling over. The policy has triggered widespread cancellations from power users frustrated by what they see as reduced value.

bullets.text: Cerebras AI chipmaker more than doubles on IPO debut day
bullets.summary: U.S. AI chipmaker Cerebras officially went public, with its stock surging more than 90% from its opening price on day one, marking the biggest IPO of 2026 so far. The strong market reception reflects investor enthusiasm for AI infrastructure companies.

bullets.text: Runway launches Agent to automate creative and video workflows
bullets.summary: Runway introduced Agent, a new agentic assistant on its video platform that collaborates with users throughout their creative workflows, automating and streamlining video production tasks.

bullets.text: Bumble replaces swipe feature with AI-powered matchmaking assistant
bullets.summary: Dating app Bumble is phasing out its swipe feature in favor of an AI assistant called Bee that handles matchmaking, profile optimization, and other dating guidance. The shift signals a broader move from manual selection to AI-driven discovery in dating apps.

bullets.text: xAI launches Grok Build CLI for developers
bullets.summary: xAI has released Grok Build, a command-line interface tool designed to help developers integrate Grok AI into their projects. The tool aims to streamline the development workflow for building applications powered by Grok's language capabilities.

bullets.text: Codex gets easier automation and customization
bullets.summary: OpenAI has enhanced Codex with improved automation features and customization options tailored to individual codebases. These updates make it easier for developers to adapt Codex behavior to their specific development environments and workflows.

bullets.text: SpaceXAI bleeding staff following merger
bullets.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceXAI has experienced significant staff departures since its recent merger. The organization has struggled to retain talent during the integration process, raising questions about retention and company culture post-merger.

bullets.text: Cursor introduces cloud agent development environments
bullets.summary: Cursor has announced cloud-based agent development environments for building AI-powered applications. This new infrastructure allows developers to create and test intelligent agents in managed cloud environments without local setup complexity.

bullets.text: Anthropic outlines 2028 AI leadership scenarios
bullets.summary: Anthropic has published research exploring potential scenarios for global AI leadership through 2028. The analysis examines competitive dynamics and technological developments that could shape the AI industry's future landscape.

bullets.text: Video-to-HTML skill transforms screen recordings into structured feedback
bullets.summary: Ben created a workflow where he records himself giving feedback on agent work, then converts the video into an HTML document with transcription and keyframe-based GIFs. The agent reads the visual report and generates an action checklist, creating a clear feedback loop that's archived for reference.

bullets.text: Claude pricing changes for third-party tool integrations starting June
bullets.summary: Anthropic is implementing separate monthly credits for Claude usage through third-party tools like Conductor and Zed, distinct from direct Claude app usage. The credits equal plan value but won't roll over, with overage billing at API rates—though they're increasing weekly limits by 50% for two months as a transition.

bullets.text: Google launches Gemini Intelligence with form auto-complete and voice-to-text
bullets.summary: Ahead of Google I/O, Gemini gained new Android features including form autofill, converting rambling voice notes to polished text, and app automations. Google also announced Googlebooks, a new laptop class, though it's not related to Google Books.

bullets.text: Notion launches developer platform with markdown API and agent integration
bullets.summary: Notion's new developer platform includes a markdown API, ability to sync external data into Notion, custom tools for Notion Agents, and infrastructure for running code. They also released a CLI called ntn, and plan to bring agents like Claude into Notion as teammates.

bullets.text: Vercel's AI Gateway index shows Anthropic leads spend, agents dominate usage
bullets.summary: Data from real production apps shows Anthropic (61% spend via Opus) and Google (38% token volume via Flash) lead different metrics, with agentic workloads representing 59% of all token consumption. Most large teams route across multiple model providers rather than committing to one.

bullets.text: U.S. and China establish AI safety protocol
bullets.summary: Treasury Secretary Bessent told CNBC that the U.S. and China are setting up safety protocols during Trump's Beijing summit, focusing on preventing frontier AI models from reaching nonstate actors like criminal networks or extremist groups. The move reflects concerns about upcoming capability jumps from Google and OpenAI releases.

bullets.text: OpenAI releases Codex mobile app for developers
bullets.summary: Codex arrived on iOS and Android as a ChatGPT feature preview, allowing developers to review and approve coding tasks remotely from their phones. The tool supports hooks and goals—automated rules and finish-line definitions—that let agents work longer without constant human prompts.

bullets.text: Marvel layoffs signal Hollywood's shift to AI and freelancers
bullets.summary: Former Disney animator Tom Bancroft highlighted that Marvel's layoffs reflect industry-wide moves toward freelance talent and AI tools instead of traditional pipelines. The trend shows how creative studios are restructuring around automation.

bullets.text: Cerebras chip IPO mints billionaires amid AI boom
bullets.summary: The AI chip company Cerebras priced shares at $185, opened at $350, and closed up 68% at $311.07 on debut day. The strong performance suggests AI hardware companies are racing to public markets as compute demand accelerates.

bullets.text: Figure robots impress with dexterity and occasional chaos
bullets.summary: Figure's latest robots demonstrated mesmerizing assembly-line work, though they sometimes randomly toss items off conveyors. The videos showcase how robotics is becoming both more capable and still delightfully unpredictable.

main_story.title: OpenAI brings Codex to mobile with iOS app launch
main_story.body: OpenAI released Codex in preview on the ChatGPT iOS app, giving developers the ability to monitor, manage, and intervene in long-running AI agent tasks directly from their phones. The mobile interface provides access to live agent threads, code changes, approvals, and the ability to launch new tasks—all while agents continue grinding away on desktop or remote systems. OpenAI emphasized its secure relay layer that doesn't expose home computers to the internet and maintains sync across different ChatGPT instances. The company's blog announcement appeared to take a direct jab at Anthropic's competing Remote Control and Dispatch features, positioning Codex mobile as offering more comprehensive task management than just remote control.
main_story.why: As AI agents improve their ability to run for hours unattended, the ability to manage them on-the-go becomes a significant quality-of-life improvement for developers. This removes the need to stay tethered to a desk or carry around open laptops in public—a practice that has become increasingly common as agent workloads grow.
main_story.take: Codex mobile represents OpenAI's play to lock in developers by making long-running agents more practical and accessible. The implicit criticism of Anthropic's approach suggests OpenAI sees this as a key differentiator in the increasingly competitive AI coding agent market.

around_the_horn.text: xAI released Grok Build beta, an agentic CLI for SuperGrok Heavy subscribers
Higgsfield AI launched Supercomputer, a cloud agent orchestrating multiple models for creative tasks
Notion opened its Developer Platform to allow building directly on Notion's infrastructure
Recraft released V4.1 with improved photorealism and illustration capabilities
Adaption launched AutoScientist to automate AI model training workflows
